title: Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual
caption:
Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual, 2023

Data from the Global Flourishing Study (2024)

This stacked horizontal bar chart displays the distribution of self-identified religious and spiritual orientations across 25 countries in 2023. The horizontal axis represents the percentage of the population, ranging from 0% to 100%, while the vertical axis lists the countries. Each bar is segmented into five categories: "Religious" (dark blue), "Both religious and spiritual" (light green), "Spiritual" (dark green), "Neither religious nor spiritual" (pink), and "Did not answer/'I don't know'" (grey).

India shows the highest share of people identifying as "Religious," with the dark blue segment extending past the 60% mark. Nigeria and Turkey also show large "Religious" segments, both exceeding 50%. In contrast, Japan has the smallest "Religious" segment, appearing to be under 10%. The "Both religious and spiritual" category is prominent in countries like the Philippines and Tanzania, where the light green segments are substantial. The "Spiritual" category, represented by dark green, is the dominant segment in the United States and Australia, occupying roughly 40% of the bar in each. The "Neither religious nor spiritual" category, shown in pink, is largest in Japan, Sweden, and the United Kingdom, where it comprises over half of the total bar length. In Germany and Spain, this pink segment also accounts for a significant portion, exceeding 50%. The grey segment for "Did not answer" remains small across all countries, generally appearing as a thin sliver at the end of the bars. The data shows a wide variation in how populations categorize their beliefs, with some nations dominated by traditional religious identification and others by secular or purely spiritual identities.
